news.title: Warsh Brings 'Rate Cut + Balance Sheet Reduction'? Senate Hearing Analysis
news.desc: Kevin Warsh attended Senate confirmation hearing on April 21, 2026. He emphasized Fed independence, regime change in monetary policy framework, and staying in the Fed's lane. On rate cuts, Warsh was cautious, refusing to give a definite timeline while tying rate questions to balance sheet issues. On balance sheet, he criticized past QE rounds and stressed the necessity of balance sheet reduction. GF Macro expects balance sheet reduction pace to be slow with no substantial new QT plan in the near term; rate cut probability is higher than balance sheet reduction.

news.title: Fed Director Miran: Prospects for Shrinking the Fed's Balance Sheet
news.desc: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran delivered a speech at the Miami Economic Club on March 26, 2026, summarizing the working paper 'A User's Guide to Reducing the Federal Reserve's Balance Sheet'. He argued for reducing the Fed's balance sheet size to minimize market distortions, reduce mark-to-market losses, and better protect the boundary between monetary and fiscal policy. The paper challenges the conventional wisdom that 'the ceiling for balance sheet reduction is reserves depletion', suggesting policy frameworks can be reshaped to achieve significant balance sheet reduction.

news.title: US Economy April 2026 Analysis & Policy Outlook
news.desc: CUHK SFI's analysis of US economy in April 2026: March CPI rose 0.9% MoM, YoY returned to 3.3%; core CPI YoY at 2.6%. Energy prices surged 10.9% MoM driven by Middle East conflict. Core PCE YoY at 2.97%. Non-farm payrolls added 178K in March with unemployment at 4.3%. The Fed is expected to hold rates at 3.50%-3.75% at the April meeting. Geopolitical tensions easing pushed oil prices down 9% to ~$94/barrel (Brent). Liquidity implications: inflation pressures complicate rate cut timeline, while TGA fluctuations remain a key liquidity driver.
